web_results.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC) - Wikipedia
web_results.description: The third round took place in New Zealand on 21 and 24 March 2025. The winner qualified for the 2026 FIFA World Cup and the runner-up advanced to the inter-confederation play-offs.

web_results.title: New Zealand thump New Caledonia to qualify for 2026 FIFA World Cup
web_results.description: New Zealand qualified for the FIFA World Cup for their third time with a 3-0 home win over New Caledonia on Monday (24 March). A trio of second-half goals by Michael Boxall, Kosta Barbarouses and Elijah Just at Auckland’s Eden Park did it ...

news_results.title: ENG vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026: England pull off late heist to stun New Zealand in Super 8
news_results.description: The England cricket team beat New Zealand by four wickets in their final Super 8 match of the T20 World Cup 2026. They qualified for the semi-finals from Group 2 as the top-ranked team.

news_results.title: Jacks and Ahmed find dramatic late blitz to earn England unlikely win over New Zealand | T20 World Cup 2026 | The Guardian
news_results.description: England made it three wins from three in the T20 World Cup Super 8s with a superb victory against New Zealand, achieved with three ball to spare

news_results.title: England vs New Zealand Highlights, T20 World Cup 2026 Super 8: Will Jacks, Rehan Ahmed's Late Burst Powers England To 4-Wicket Win vs NZ | Cricket News
news_results.description: England vs New Zealand Highlights: Already qualified for semifinals, England beat New Zealand by four wickets to top Super Eights Group 2 of T20 World Cup.

news_results.title: New Zealand await semi-final fate after England's thrilling win | ICC Men's T20 World Cup, 2026 | ICC
news_results.description: England continued their winning run with a four-wicket victory over the Black Caps in their final Super Eight clash.

news_results.title: FIFA to 'monitor developments' in Iran ahead of 2026 World Cup - ESPN
news_results.description: FIFA will "monitor developments" in Iran following the outbreak of military action by the United States -- the co-hosts of this summer's men's World Cup -- against the Middle Eastern nation.
